dublinCore.dc:title: 107 HR 5894 IH: Renewing Efforts to Suspend Testing and Reinforce Arms-control Initiatives Now Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Titus introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To amend the Atomic Energy Defense Act to prohibit explosive testing of nuclear weapons conducted by the United States, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Renewing Efforts to Suspend Testing and Reinforce Arms-control Initiatives Now Act or the RESTRAIN Act.

section.header: Prohibition on explosive testing of nuclear weapons conducted by the United States
section.text: Section 4210 of the Atomic Energy Defense Act (division D of the Bob Stump National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2003 (Public Law 107–314; 50 U.S.C. 2530)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking subsection (a) and inserting the following:

subsection.text: No explosive testing of a nuclear weapon, or any other nuclear explosion, may be conducted by the United States.

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following new subsections:

subsection.text: None of the funds authorized to be appropriated by an Act authorizing funds for fiscal year 2026 or otherwise made available for any fiscal year may be used—

paragraph.text: to conduct explosive testing of a nuclear weapon; or
Nothing in this section may be constructed to limit the authority of the United States to conduct a subcritical nuclear test.
In this subsection, the term subcritical nuclear test means a nuclear test of fissile materials that are not capable of sustaining an explosive nuclear chain reaction.
